findings.title: CI validate-template.yml still failing — fixes exist locally but not committed/pushed
evidence: checksums.yaml:15 — __pycache__ directory entry (old .pyc entry removed, fix in working tree)
gh run list: 5/5 recent CI runs showing 'conclusion: failure'
test-checksums.sh local: orphan check passes (0 orphans)
findings.why: The P0-BUG-01 fix (removing orphan .pyc checksum entry + adding __pycache__ to never_touch) resolved the local failure, but the changes are in the working tree only. The CI tests the committed/pushed version which still has the old checksums.yaml. This is a process gap: local fixes that aren't committed don't flow to CI.
findings.source: SWE@Google Ch.11 — CI gates must reflect current state; divergence between local and CI state erodes trust
findings.fix_sketch: 1. Commit checksums.yaml fix. 2. Commit new test files (test-lib-env.sh, test-lib-telegram.sh). 3. Commit other modified files. 4. Push. 5. Verify CI passes.
findings.cross_reference: Update of P0-CI-01 (from audit #3): P0-BUG-01 is fixed locally, but CI is still failing because fix isn't propagated

findings.title: || true and 2>/dev/null instances still growing — process anti-pattern
evidence: || true count: 175 (was 174, +1 since audit #3, was 16 in audit #1)
Top || true offenders: test-ai-cli-wrapper.sh:12, test-extension-points.sh:8, canary-wp-gate.sh:6
findings.why: Growth slowed significantly (only +1 || true, +25 2>/dev/null vs +18 for each in previous audit), but the trend is still upward. Each instance is a potential silent failure point. While Bash scripts legitimately need some error suppression (command not found guards, optional tools), the aggregate count of 911 suppressions in a ~55-file test suite is high. This is the 'auto-retry as flake mitigation' anti-pattern in a different form: instead of fixing why errors occur, they're suppressed.
findings.source: Google Testing Blog Flaky Tests series; bash manual §Shell Builtin Commands (command substitution without exit check is a failure-mode antipattern)
findings.fix_sketch: 1. Set a cap: no new || true or 2>/dev/null without comment explaining why. 2. Audit top-10 offenders each week. 3. Replace 2>/dev/null with explicit stderr capture + display-on-failure pattern (as done in run-e2e-ai.sh fix).

findings.title: lib/lib-telegram.sh:42 masks python3 JSON parse errors with 2>/dev/null
evidence: lib/lib-telegram.sh:38-40 — response=$(curl --fail ...) — curl failures produce empty response
lib/lib-telegram.sh:43 — [[ "$ok" == "True" ]] — correctly returns 1 on failure (behavior is correct)
findings.why: The function correctly returns exit code 1 when curl fails or the API returns non-'ok' (verified by test-lib-telegram.sh:125-129, 149-153). However, the 2>/dev/null on python3 swallows the diagnostic error message. If python3 is not installed, the script fails silently — the user gets 'send failed' with no clue whether the issue is network, token, or missing python3. This is a Command Substitution Without Exit Check failure-mode antipattern at P2 severity because the rc propagates correctly — only diagnostics are lost.
findings.source: bash manual §Shell Builtin Commands; failure-injection-gate.md §Common Failure Modes
findings.fix_sketch: Replace 2>/dev/null with explicit stderr capture to temp file, display on failure. Or add a python3 availability check at function entry with clear error message.

findings.title: 6 modified files + 2 new test files untracked — changes exist in working tree only
evidence: git status: M checksums.yaml, M lib/lib-telegram.sh, M scripts/generate-checksums.sh, M scripts/test/canary-wp-gate.sh, M scripts/test/e2e/run-e2e-ai.sh, M scripts/test/run-phase0.sh
git status: ?? scripts/test/test-lib-env.sh (169 lines), ?? scripts/test/test-lib-telegram.sh (185 lines)
git status: ?? .audit/ files from audit #3, ?? CLAUDE.md.migration backup files
findings.why: All P0/P1 fixes from audit #3 are implemented but uncommitted. The new test files (355 lines) close P1-GAP-01 but are untracked. If the working directory is lost (disk failure, accidental rm), these changes are gone. More importantly, CI can't verify them until they're pushed.
findings.source: SWE@Google Ch.11 — tests are infrastructure; untracked tests are invisible to CI and the team
findings.fix_sketch: 1. Add new test files to git. 2. Commit all modified files with descriptive messages referencing findings they fix. 3. Push. 4. Remove stale backup files (CLAUDE.md.migration-0.18.0.backup, protocol-close.md.backup).
